edge_hypothesis: Supertrend at ADX>5 produced PF=1.22 (ACCEPT) with ranging PF=1.611 — the system's 2nd-best ranging performance after MACD 7:1's PF=2.06. Time caps have been tested on MACD (mc6t8b3k) but NEVER on Supertrend. This spec tests two converging hypotheses: (1) Time discipline is template-agnostic: if losers meander and winners move fast on MACD, the same should be true for Supertrend, especially in ranging markets where reversals are FAST by nature — price bounces off range boundaries quickly. A Supertrend direction flip in a range should produce a fast move to the opposite boundary or fail within 4-6 bars. (2) Low-ADX Supertrend captures range reversals: when ADX is low (5-10), Supertrend flips direction at range extremes. These aren't trend-following signals — they're MEAN-REVERSION signals disguised as trend flips. The move back toward range center should be rapid and predictable. Combined, the hypothesis is: Supertrend + low ADX + time cap = range reversal harvester. The time cap is MORE important for Supertrend-in-ranges than for MACD because ranging markets have a natural ceiling on how far price can move — the opposite range boundary. If the move doesn't reach TP within N bars, it's likely stalling mid-range. Exiting at partial profit/loss beats waiting for a full stop. Macro alignment: IntoTheCryptoverse analysis confirms we're below the logarithmic BTC trend line — a ranging/bear environment where this exact pattern should dominate. Invalidation: if PF drops below 1.0 with time caps, Supertrend direction flips in ranges produce slow, grinding moves rather than sharp reversals — time caps are cutting the gradual winners that eventually reach TP.

variants.name: supertrend_1h_ranging_timecap5_7to1
variants.description: Supertrend direction flip on ETH/1h with ADX>5 (ranging-inclusive), 7:1 R:R, and 5-bar time cap (5 hours). ADX>5 = virtually all market conditions, heavily weighted toward ranging. At 7:1 R:R with 5-bar cap, this tests whether Supertrend reversals in ranges produce fast moves. The 5-bar cap on 1h is aggressive — only the sharpest range reversals will reach 14x ATR TP within 5 hours. Most trades will exit at the time cap, creating a distribution of partial wins and partial losses. If the average time-cap exit is profitable (price moved favorably but not to full TP), PF will improve vs no time cap because the losers also exit early at smaller losses. Previous Supertrend ADX>5 at 6:1 produced 99 trades. At 7:1 with time cap, expect similar trade count but different P&L distribution.
entry_long: SUPERTd_7_3.0 flips from negative to positive (Supertrend direction change to bullish).
ADX_14 > 5 (minimal trend filter — allows ranging market entries).
entry_short: SUPERTd_7_3.0 flips from positive to negative (Supertrend direction change to bearish).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (14.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Exit at market after 5 bars if neither TP nor SL has been hit (time-decay exit — 5 hours on 1h).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < 1.0, range reversals need more time to develop — try 8-bar cap
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 14.0x ATR_14 from entry.
Time exit after 5 bars at market price.

components.notes: SUPERTd_7_3.0 direction flip — range reversal signal
ADX_14 > 5 — minimal filter, ranging-inclusive for mean-reversion captures
ATR_14 for 2.0x stop / 14.0x TP — 7:1 R:R with 5-bar time cap

variants.name: supertrend_1h_ranging_timecap8_8to1
variants.description: Supertrend direction flip on ETH/1h with ADX>10, 8:1 R:R, and 8-bar time cap (8 hours). ADX>10 is slightly more selective than ADX>5 — still captures ranging markets but filters out the absolute flattest conditions where Supertrend may whipsaw. The 8-bar cap gives more room than the 5-bar variant while still enforcing time discipline. At 8:1 R:R (16x ATR TP), this pushes into the R:R frontier for Supertrend — existing ACCEPT was at 6:1, and st7a3k5w tests up to 12:1 but without time caps. This variant isolates the interaction between moderate time discipline + higher R:R on Supertrend. Key comparison: vs 5-bar variant (same template, different time cap + R:R) to find the optimal time/R:R combination.
entry_long: SUPERTd_7_3.0 flips from negative to positive (bullish direction change).
ADX_14 > 10 (mild trend filter — excludes only flattest markets).
entry_short: SUPERTd_7_3.0 flips from positive to negative (bearish direction change).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (16.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Exit at market after 8 bars if neither TP nor SL has been hit (time-decay exit — 8 hours on 1h).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < PF of ADX>5 variant, the ADX>10 filter is removing profitable ranging reversals
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 16.0x ATR_14 from entry.
Time exit after 8 bars at market price.

variants.name: supertrend_1h_ranging_nocap_7to1_control
variants.description: Control variant: Supertrend on ETH/1h with ADX>5, 7:1 R:R, NO time cap. This isolates the time cap effect. Existing ACCEPT was ADX>5 at 6:1 (PF=1.22). This variant tests the same ADX threshold at 7:1 without time cap. The A/B comparison between this control and the 5-bar timecap variant directly answers: does time cap improve Supertrend in ranging markets? If timecap PF > no-cap PF, time discipline is confirmed for Supertrend. If no-cap PF > timecap PF, Supertrend trends develop slowly and need open-ended duration — time caps are MACD-specific.

entry_short: SUPERTd_7_3.0 flips from positive to negative (bearish direction change).
